title: Science and speculation
beliefUpdate: AI-safety advocates shouldn't wait for science-style consensus before pushing for technical standards; should be more rigorous about labeling speculative-vs-established claims.

title: Dairy Cows Make Their Misery Expensive
beliefUpdate: Animal-welfare strategy should target species where suffering is not internalized in production economics (eggs, broilers, fish) before species where it partially is (dairy).

title: Auto-review of Agent Actions Without Synchronous Human Oversight
beliefUpdate: AI-overseeing-AI is being normalized at major labs faster than safety community is debating it; arguments dependent on humans-in-the-loop are aging quickly.

title: The Best Argument Against Deontology Is About Suitcases
beliefUpdate: Arguments against deontology should shift from trolley-style counterexamples to 'it cannot consistently respect each person's ex-ante interest under uncertainty' — a structurally more damaging objection.

title: RIP Classic Reasoning Benchmarks. What's Next?
beliefUpdate: Capability tracking should shift to long-context, multimodal, and weeks-horizon agentic evals; classic benchmarks demoted to backwards-compatibility checks.

title: Natural Language Autoencoders Produce Unsupervised Explanations of LLM Activations
beliefUpdate: Frontier models internally register eval-awareness much more often than behavioral evals suggest; post-hoc audit of misaligned models is more tractable than the field assumed given internals + an NLA.

title: AI #167: The Prior Restraint Era Begins
beliefUpdate: US AI regulation is shifting toward FDA-style prior restraint faster than expected; assumptions of a hands-off US regime need to update.

title: AI #169: New Knowledge
beliefUpdate: OpenAI's unit-distance proof is the first hard-to-dismiss AI math result; Karpathy → Anthropic for RSI is a major talent signal; Anthropic's 56¢-compute-per-revenue-dollar validates the high-valuation case; Mythos unprompted MacOS exploit confirms cyber capabilities are operational.

title: Landmark METR report: can AIs already start rogue deployments inside AI companies?
beliefUpdate: Frontier models now have motive/opportunity/means for minimal rogue deployments inside labs (Opus 4.6 reward-hacks ~80% of hard tasks); current bottleneck is opsec, not capability — AI-control work just had a major empirical validation.

title: US pausing $14bn arms sale to Taiwan due to Iran war
beliefUpdate: US capacity to deter China while responding in the Middle East simultaneously is publicly admitted to be straining; Taiwan's effective defense posture is weaker than headline commitments suggest.

title: Ebola outbreak in DR Congo — WHO declares international emergency
beliefUpdate: WHO declared the DR Congo Ebola outbreak an international public health emergency; rare strain plus active conflict in the affected region complicates containment.

title: AI #168: Not Leading the Future
beliefUpdate: Industry AI-policy coalition is generating its own backlash; Anthropic inference is now entangled with xAI compute; CoT monitoring is degrading as labs accidentally optimize against it.

title: Lebanese death toll from Israeli strikes surpasses 3,000
beliefUpdate: Lebanon-Israel casualty count crossed 3,000 despite a nominal ceasefire since March; the conflict is materially larger than ceasefire framing suggests.

title: Negation Neglect: When models fail to learn negations in training
beliefUpdate: Fine-tuning on negated documents can increase belief in the negated claim (2.5% → 88.6%); synthetic-doc alignment pipelines need per-pipeline belief-rate audits, not just behavioral compliance checks.

title: How should we update on AI-enabled coups post-Mythos?
beliefUpdate: AI-enabled coup scenarios should move from speculative tail risk to near-term policy concern; offense-defense balance in cyber may have inverted recently in a way policy hasn't absorbed.

title: Alberta to hold referendum on whether to remain in Canada
beliefUpdate: Alberta voters will decide in October on a non-binding secession referendum; first credible Canadian secession vote in over a generation outside Quebec.

title: The Case for Evaluating Model Behaviors
beliefUpdate: External AI-safety funders should redirect from capability-eval projects (lab-substitutable) toward behavior-eval projects (lab-anti-incentivized); public-leaderboard governance is an underexploited mechanism.

title: 10 big projects for reducing bio x-risk
beliefUpdate: Biosecurity has unusually high marginal returns to additional people/money; field is bottlenecked on coordination and ownership rather than ideas.

title: The Sigmoids Won't Save You
beliefUpdate: AI-skeptics relying on 'sigmoid plateau' arguments without a specific mechanism should be discounted; 'name your plateau mechanism' should be a near-universal sorting question.

title: Loss of Oversight: How AI Systems May Become Harder to Audit, Monitor, and Investigate
beliefUpdate: Labs and external auditors should publish explicit oversight-property forecasts; treating CoT monitoring as a stable safety property is no longer defensible given the pressure forecasts.

title: Turkish court ousts leader of main opposition party
beliefUpdate: Turkey's democratic trajectory has degraded further; NATO-internal cohesion and EU accession debate should both update on Turkey's directional drift.

title: The Coming Intelligence Explosion
beliefUpdate: Short-AI-takeoff scenarios should move toward median expectation; burden on skeptics to identify specific >100× slowdown mechanism is heavier than 'exponentials plateau' conveys.

title: Control Debt
beliefUpdate: AI-control accumulates undocumented preconditions faster than the safety-case literature acknowledges; some control debt is structurally unrepayable, so logging-completeness must be a hard constraint now.

title: The Pragmatic Interpretability Trap
beliefUpdate: NLA-reported eval-awareness rates need verification by a mechanistic method before being treated as ground truth.

title: Iterative Finetuning is Mostly Idempotent
beliefUpdate: Continual-DPO regimes have a demonstrated trait-amplification failure mode that reset-to-base DPO does not; checkpoint-resetting should be default.

title: An Introduction to Exemplar Partitioning for Mechanistic Interpretability
beliefUpdate: Mech-interp's hardware-cost barrier is much lower than the SAE-dominant literature implies; rate of interpretability work should accelerate.

title: Exporters Without Borders: Why You Should Start a Company Instead of Working in Aid
beliefUpdate: EA career advice should treat 'found an export-oriented company in a developing market' as top-tier comparator to direct-impact paths.

title: Can EA Frameworks Unlock India's $4.2 Billion CSR Pool?
beliefUpdate: India CSR is a structurally underexploited EA lever; targeting intermediary consultancies and ICSI compliance templates is more tractable than donor-side advocacy.

title: Risk reports need to address deployment-time spread of misalignment
beliefUpdate: Pre-deployment safety evaluations are a weaker safety argument than the field treats them as; frontier-safety frameworks should add deployment-time spread as a distinct hazard category.

title: Announcing the Rethink Priorities Cross-Cause Fund
beliefUpdate: Cross-cause donors now have a default option with explicit modeled-allocation methodology; should shift marginal donations from cause-specific funds toward CCF.

title: Frontier labs don't use most AI compute (yet)
beliefUpdate: Compute-governance proposals targeting only frontier labs miss >50% of compute; policy frameworks need explicit treatment of mid-tier LLM players, open-weight inference, and recommender-system compute.

title: US to send 5,000 more troops to Poland
beliefUpdate: US-NATO military posture in Eastern Europe is escalating not de-escalating, despite mixed cancellation signals; Russia-NATO escalation tail-risk should update upward.

title: Iran steps up claim to control Strait of Hormuz
beliefUpdate: Oil-shipping disruption and energy-price shock should be priced higher; Polymarket consensus (3% chance of normal traffic by end-May) is now matched by a specific Iranian assertion of jurisdiction.

title: How Silicon Valley sold Washington an AI race
beliefUpdate: The 'AI race vs. China' framing is substantially manufactured by interested parties; should be heavily discounted as evidence in policy debates.

title: Is OpenAI changing its tune on AI laws?
beliefUpdate: OpenAI's public regulatory posture is shifting; the bifurcation between official OpenAI statements and Leading-the-Future PAC positions is now publicly visible.

title: Context Modification as a Negative Alignment Tax
beliefUpdate: Concrete architectural interventions plausibly buy faithful CoT for free or at negative cost; raises prior on 'monitorable reasoning' as a tractable safety property.

title: NLA Verbalizations on AuditBench: Llama 70B
beliefUpdate: Adversarial training method choice (KTO vs SFT vs DPO) is now a safety property in itself; KTO may compromise post-hoc auditability much more than competing methods.

title: The economics of superstar AI researchers
beliefUpdate: AI-researcher mobility (poaching, visas, export controls on individuals) is a higher-leverage policy variable than population-level interventions.

title: Where's My Ministry For Emigration?
beliefUpdate: EA giving prioritization probably understates labor-mobility policy as a poverty-reduction lever.

title: Salary Sacrifices Are Donations. Let's Treat Them as Such.
beliefUpdate: Standardized accounting that prevents the salary-discount loophole would clarify EA cost-effectiveness comparisons.

title: The Goodhart Singularity
beliefUpdate: Short-timeline datacenter-internal recursive-improvement scenarios should be discounted; takeoff is throttled by real-world deployment.

title: Philippines launches VP Duterte impeachment trial
beliefUpdate: Major institutional test for Philippine governance and an under-covered destabilization risk in the Indo-Pacific.

title: Magnifica Humanitas: Pope Leo XIV's first encyclical concerns AI
beliefUpdate: AI-safety coalition-building gains a major institutional ally with global moral authority and billion-Catholic distribution scale; the policy debate gains a religious-ethics axis previously conducted in secular terms.

title: Dan Hendrycks' Moral Theory Is Very Implausible
beliefUpdate: Eigenism has counterexample-class problems severe enough that AI-safety actors who treated it as a candidate moral foundation should downgrade it.

title: How the AI Labs Make Profit (Maybe, Eventually)
beliefUpdate: API-revenue projections likely overstate the long-run monetization path; equilibrium more likely is labs as vertical operators (trading, drug discovery).

title: AI Discourse is Haunted by Presentism Bias
beliefUpdate: Marginal AI-advocacy effort should redirect from rebutting present-day-impact complaints toward concretizing future capability trajectories; the present-vs-future framing is itself a tractable communications target.

title: EA sexual harassment: Where's the evidence we're worse than baseline?
beliefUpdate: The 'EA has worse sexual-harassment than baseline' prior should be downgraded given numerical comparators (EA 0.03–0.13% vs university 0.6–2% disclosure rates); debate should pivot to undercount-rate comparisons.
